title: Gmail Newsletter Draft
description: Reads email threads from Gmail as source material, researches the topic, drafts a newsletter, and sends it after approval. Exercises Gmail read/draft/send and DuckDuckGo search.

nodes.title: Select Email Threads
nodes.prompt: Ask the user: which Gmail threads should we use as source material? Suggest: (A) Search for recent customer emails, (B) Look for specific topic threads, (C) Let me scan your inbox for content signals. Also ask: how many threads to review?

nodes.title: Read Email Threads
nodes.prompt: Search Gmail for the relevant threads. Read the full content of each thread. Extract: topics discussed, pain points mentioned, questions asked, and content opportunities.

nodes.title: Research Topic
nodes.prompt: Research the topic signals from the email threads. Find current trends, data points, and relevant context to enrich the newsletter.

nodes.title: Write Newsletter
nodes.prompt: Write a newsletter using the email-newsletter template. The hook comes from the email thread signals. Use research for proof. Apply voice match.

nodes.title: Approve Newsletter
nodes.prompt: Show the newsletter draft. Ask: (A) Send via Gmail now, (B) Save as draft in Gmail for manual review, (C) Edit and resend, (D) Reject.

nodes.title: Send or Draft
nodes.prompt: If approved to send: create the draft and send. If approved as draft: create the draft only and mark it for manual review. Return status.
